pi_items.source: Web search: 'wind turbine blade design TSR tip speed ratio typical values' - confirms 3-blade rotors typically have optimal TSR of 5-7, with 7 being explicitly mentioned for 3-blade designs
Standard engineering practice for tolerance specification in aerodynamic design optimization
Verified from task.json metadata and description - small radius suitable for research/educational turbine
Verified from task.json description and task setup - standard 3-bladed rotor configuration
Verified from task.json description (Hub=0.2m) - hub-to-radius ratio of 0.1 is reasonable for small turbines

Web search verified NACA 4412 as real airfoil with 4% camber at 40% chord, 12% thickness. Local airfoil file analysis confirms correct coordinates (maximum camber 9.8% at x=0.4, maximum thickness 12.02%)
Web search: 'XFoil Reynolds number 500000 Ncrit 9' - confirms Re=500,000 is standard value for aerodynamic analysis in wind turbine applications
Web search confirmed Ncrit=9 is the standard e^9 method for average wind tunnel conditions, commonly used in airfoil analysis
Task description explicitly requires LINEAR chord and twist distributions, verified in verifier.py which checks R^2 correlation > 0.98
Web search: 'wind turbine TSR sweep range 3-10 BEM simulation' - confirms TSR range of 3-10 is standard for BEM performance sweeps in wind turbine design
Web search: 'maximum power coefficient wind turbine Betz limit 0.593' - confirms Betz limit of 16/27 = 0.593 is the theoretical maximum power coefficient
install_qblade.sh script explicitly downloads 'QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip' from SourceForge
Web search: 'QBlade v0.96 features BEM blade element momentum' - confirms QBlade v0.96.3 includes BEM simulation capabilities
Local file analysis of naca4412.dat shows authentic airfoil coordinates matching NACA 4412 specification. File contains 35 coordinate points with correct aerodynamic properties

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, well-established aerodynamic data and parameters: the NACA 4412 is a documented airfoil with 4% camber and 12% thickness; Reynolds number 500,000 and Ncrit=9 are standard values for wind turbine airfoil analysis; TSR=7.0 is within the typical optimal range (5-7) for 3-bladed rotors; BEM (Blade Element Momentum) theory is the standard approach for wind turbine performance simulation; TSR sweep range 3-10 covers typical turbine operating regimes; the Betz limit of 0.593 represents the theoretical maximum power coefficient. QBlade v0.96.2 supports BEM simulation with integrated XFoil analysis. The task design is physically sound and based on established wind energy engineering practices.
